finding: Local MaaS and multi-platform high-fee effects cannot be cleanly separated from platform maturity
evidence: For local MaaS, early and mid maturity segments have zero observations with unlock_fee_ge_1_00 or unlock_fee_ge_1_20; high-fee observations occur only in mature_366_plus rows (90 rows for EUR 1.00, 72 rows for EUR 1.20). For multi-platform, early and mid maturity segments also have zero high-fee observations; high-fee observations occur only in mature_366_plus rows (1308 rows for EUR 1.00, 1029 rows for EUR 1.20).
interpretation: The high-unlock-fee Local MaaS/Multi-platform finding is best interpreted as a mature-platform pricing-friction effect, not as evidence that maturity independently strengthens the fee penalty.
caveat: This is an identification limitation, not a falsification of the threshold result.

finding: Large aggregator maturity does not support a stronger high-fee penalty over time
evidence: For large aggregator membership, high-fee variation exists in early and mature segments. The maturity triple is positive: unlock_fee_ge_1_00:large_aggregator_only:mature_366_plus has all market FE = 0.197 (p < 0.001) and all unit FE = 0.083 (p < 0.001); unlock_fee_ge_1_20 triple has all market FE = 0.228 (p < 0.001) and all unit FE = 0.074 (p < 0.001).
interpretation: For Free Now-style large aggregator exposure, high unlock fees appear more detrimental in early exposure than in mature exposure; this is inconsistent with a simple learning-over-time penalty story.
caveat: This applies to large aggregator exposure and should not be generalized to Local MaaS/Multi-platform, where maturity and high-fee exposure are confounded.
